question: Which of the following represent external challenge(s) for a company?

options.C: the use of green technology to reduce the negative impact of external challenges

options.A: production planning for the main production lines
options.B: determining the quantity to be manufactured for each model made on a production line
options.C: detailed planning of the material requirements for each model made on the production line
options.D: the human resource planning involved in the production process

question: Which of the following is/are component(s) of a CIM system?
The use of group technology decreases the number of operations in the production process.
Assembly to order is used when:

options.A: the combination of product options can't be predicted statistically
options.B: subassemblies and components for the finished product are available in stock
options.C: the time the customer is willing to wait allows the product to be made after receiving the order
options.D: the time the customer is willing to wait is less than the manufacturing time
options.E: the product is in the first stage on the life cycle curve

options.A: which products are to be manufactured
options.C: how much of which will be produced

question: The transformation of the raw material from one state to another, the latter being closer to the desired component or finished product is carried out by:

options.A: increased production costs in the case of CM systems
options.B: increased production costs in the case of classical manufacturing systems
options.C: decreased production costs in the case of classical manufacturing systems

question: Cycle stock exists when the production rate exceeds the sales (or demand) rate.
Group technology can be applied only when the products share similarities.
Guidance based on painted strips is the most indicated technology.

options.B: the number of operations required for manufacturing

question: Dedicated storage can't be used when products are stored according to the level of activity.
